name: pptx
description: Presentation creation, editing, and analysis. When Claude needs to work with presentations (.pptx files) for: (1) Creating new presentations, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with layouts, (4) Adding comments or speaker notes, or any other presentation tasks
capabilities: Create new presentations from HTML using the html2pptx library
Edit existing presentations by manipulating OOXML structure
Generate visual thumbnail grids for slide analysis
Apply template designs by rearranging and replacing content
Extract text inventory with formatting preserved
Convert presentations to PDF and images for visual review
author: Cam10001110101
securitySummary: Legitimate PowerPoint manipulation skill. All static findings are false positives: markdown code blocks flagged as shell execution, XSD schema namespace URLs flagged as hardcoded URLs, and Office XML element names flagged as C2 keywords. The skill uses documented subprocess calls (soffice, pdftoppm) for legitimate document conversion. No credential exfiltration, no unauthorized network access, no code injection vectors.
valueStatement: Create, edit, and analyze PowerPoint files with comprehensive tools for template-based design, HTML-to-PPTX conversion, and visual thumbnail preview generation.
limitations: Cannot edit embedded media or complex animations directly
Requires LibreOffice and Poppler utilities installed for conversions
Template-based creation requires analyzing existing template structure first

useCases.title: Create presentations from templates
useCases.description: Duplicate and customize professional templates by rearranging slides and replacing placeholder content with your own text.

useCases.title: Build presentations from HTML
useCases.description: Convert HTML slides with CSS styling to PowerPoint format using the html2pptx workflow for pixel-perfect design control.

useCases.title: Edit existing presentations
useCases.description: Modify presentation content by unpacking the OOXML structure, editing XML directly, and repacking the file.

promptTemplates.title: Create new presentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new PowerPoint presentation with 5 slides about [TOPIC]. Use the html2pptx workflow with professional styling.

promptTemplates.title: Use template
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a presentation using [TEMPLATE_FILE.pptx] as a template. Rearrange slides to show [SLIDE_ORDER]. Replace text with [NEW_CONTENT].

promptTemplates.title: Extract content
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract the text inventory from [PRESENTATION.pptx] and save it to a JSON file. Read the full content to understand the structure.

promptTemplates.title: Visual validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a thumbnail grid for [PRESENTATION.pptx] and check for any text cutoff, overlap, or positioning issues across all slides.

bestPractices: Always validate presentations after editing OOXML structure using the validate.py script
Generate thumbnail grids to visually verify slide layouts before finalizing
Read full documentation files before using html2pptx or ooxml editing workflows
antiPatterns: Avoid skipping the validation step after editing XML files
Do not use html2pptx for simple presentations that would be faster with template duplication
Do not forget to check thumbnail grids for layout issues before finalizing

faq.question: Can I edit presentations without Microsoft PowerPoint?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill manipulates the OOXML format directly. You can unpack, edit XML, and repack presentations on any system.

faq.question: What dependencies are required?
faq.answer: LibreOffice (soffice), Poppler (pdftoppm), markitdown, pptxgenjs, and playwright are required for full functionality.

faq.question: How do I fix layout issues?
faq.answer: Generate thumbnail grids to identify issues, then adjust HTML margins and spacing for html2pptx or modify XML directly.

faq.question: Can I create charts and tables?
faq.answer: Yes, use PptxGenJS API within html2pptx for charts. Tables can be created by replacing placeholder shapes with formatted text.

faq.question: How do I preserve template styling?
faq.answer: Analyze the template inventory first, then use the same placeholder positions and paragraph formatting properties in replacements.

faq.question: What file operations are safe?
faq.answer: All documented operations (unpack, validate, pack, inventory, replace, thumbnail) are designed for safe document manipulation.
